review: {"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/pipeline.py", "line_number": 785, "severity": "medium", "issue": "Infrastructure-only routing fallback violates the Open/Closed Principle. The hardcoded _INFRASTRUCTURE_AGENTS set is tightly coupled to the fallback logic. When new infrastructure-only agents are added (e.g., 'docs-validator'), the fallback logic must be modified. This creates a maintenance burden and risk of inconsistency between agent classification and fallback behavior.", "suggestion": "Extract infrastructure agent classification into a dedicated abstraction. Define a method on AgentDefinition or a separate AgentClassifier that determines if an agent is infrastructure-only. This allows agent definitions to declare their classification without modifying pipeline logic. Pattern: Strategy pattern for agent classification.", "confidence": 0.85, "category": "architecture", "agent_name": "leo"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/pipeline.py", "line_number": 787, "severity": "medium", "issue": "Infrastructure-only fallback silently ignores routing decision without logging the original route. If Cleo intentionally routed to infrastructure-only agents (e.g., nova for YAML validation), the fallback to all reviewers bypasses that decision and may waste compute on unnecessary reviews.", "suggestion": "Log the original routing decision before fallback, or add a flag to distinguish between 'Cleo returned empty' (fallback appropriate) vs 'Cleo returned infrastructure-only' (fallback may be wrong). Example: `print(f'[revue]   Original route: {[a.name for a in reviewer_agents]}'); print('[revue]   Fallback: infrastructure-only routing...')`", "confidence": 0.72, "category": "code-quality", "agent_name": "maya"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/ai_client.py", "line_number": 71, "severity": "low", "issue": "The agent_name parameter is added to the AIClient Protocol but is optional (default None). This creates an implicit contract where callers may omit agent_name, leading to silent data loss in metrics (as evidenced by REVUE-162). The protocol should document whether agent_name is required for correct metrics collection.", "suggestion": "Add a docstring to the AIClient.complete() method clarifying that agent_name should be provided by callers to enable per-agent metrics breakdown. Consider making it a required parameter if all call sites can provide it, or document the None case explicitly in MetricsEvent schema.", "confidence": 0.72, "category": "architecture", "agent_name": "leo"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/tests/core/test_pipeline.py", "line_number": 1655, "severity": "low", "issue": "Test mocks `route()` to return `[nova]` (infrastructure-only), but the fallback logic checks `if not reviewer_agents:` (empty list). The test name and docstring claim to test 'infrastructure-only routing falls back', but the mock setup doesn't match that scenario.", "suggestion": "Change the mock to return an empty list: `route=MagicMock(return_value=(MagicMock(), []))` to test the actual empty-list fallback condition. If you want to test infrastructure-only fallback separately, add a second test with `[nova]` and verify that non-infrastructure agents are excluded from the fallback.", "confidence": 0.68, "category": "code-quality", "agent_name": "maya"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/agent_loader.py", "line_number": 153, "severity": "low", "issue": "LoadedAgent.analyse() passes agent_name to complete(), but the parameter flows through the AIClient protocol without validation. If a subclass of AIClient ignores the agent_name parameter (as AnthropicClient did before this fix), the bug silently propagates. There is no contract enforcement that agent_name must be used.", "suggestion": "Add an assertion or type-level contract in the AIClient protocol that agent_name, when provided, must be stored in the returned CompletionResult or MetricsEvent. Alternatively, add a post-call validation in LoadedAgent that verifies agent_name was recorded in metrics.", "confidence": 0.68, "category": "architecture", "agent_name": "leo"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/tests/core/test_ai_client.py", "line_number": 650, "severity": "low", "issue": "Test assertion `assert event.agent_name is None  # not passed \u2192 None` documents the current behavior but does not validate the AC5 contract requirement.
